top_viral_mechanic: Deep, data-literate credibility signal: 'I spotted the algo recovering from rank 200 to rank 50 in 30 days' is genuinely smart-person proof-of-work in r/algotrading, not hype
biggest_weakness: Zero invitation loop and no public evidence trail—users consume this privately; no reason to screenshot, share, or recruit others into stockarithm to use it better

top_viral_mechanic: Enabling skeptical traders to publicly prove 'the algo isn't curve-fit' by sharing audit modal screenshots on r/algotrading, leveraging Stockarithm's transparency-as-moat positioning.
biggest_weakness: Feature is read-only inspection with no sharing mechanism, so proof-of-work stays private unless user manually screenshots and posts; lacks inherent invitation loop (no reason to invite others for personal audit access).

top_viral_mechanic: Operator's written reasoning becomes public artifact that proves transparency and intellectual honesty — exactly what r/algotrading respects and shares as 'look how candid this founder is about failures'
biggest_weakness: Zero network effects or invitation loops; one user's thesis doesn't make the product better for others, and theses don't incentivize sign-ups

top_viral_mechanic: Timeline visualization of algo reclassifications creates sharable 'receipts' of which bets paid off and which failed, turning classification changes into micro-narratives the skeptical r/algotrading crowd will screenshot and debate.
biggest_weakness: Feature is inherently introspective—users consume it individually on detail pages; there is no incentive to share a migration log, no social loop, and no competitive element that forces cross-user visibility or invitations.

top_viral_mechanic: Weaponizes transparency against fintech hype culture—users can screenshot a leaderboard with prominent loss flags and share as proof of honesty, directly contrasting with hidden-losses competitors.
biggest_weakness: No incentive for users to invite others or loop back; the feature educates existing skeptical users but doesn't create FOMO or collaborative advantage for network growth.

top_viral_mechanic: Leverages stockarithm's unique failures-published transparency by making bucket classification (ALPHA/noise/unresolved) visible, which reinforces the legitimacy story to skeptical r/algotrading audiences.
biggest_weakness: Pure UX refinement with zero sharing incentives—users filtering signals locally gains them nothing to broadcast, no reason to invite others, and creates no public evidence of their success.

top_viral_mechanic: Niche appeal to data-literate skeptics who can claim discovery of unresolved patterns; fits stockarithm's anti-hype DNA perfectly.
biggest_weakness: No sharing mechanic, no social proof layer, and divergence spotting is inherently a solitary research activity with zero incentive to loop others in.

top_viral_mechanic: Raw, unflinching trade receipts with red losses prominent—speaks directly to skeptical r/algotrading ethos and proves transparency claims with hard data.
biggest_weakness: Zero incentive to share or invite others; a Receipts Guy auditing trades alone doesn't broadcast that audit socially, so it generates no public footprint or network growth.
